_comment: Source: Michigan MDHHS Section 2-5 Soft Tissue and Orthopedic Injuries (Revised 08/11/2023, MDHHS Reviewed 2023). Base Michigan template — MCA-specific selections not configured.
protocol_reference: Michigan Trauma and Environmental Protocol Section 2-5: Soft Tissue and Orthopedic Injuries — MDHHS Approved 8/11/23, Reviewed 2023

sections.title: Soft Tissue & Orthopedic Injuries
sections.reference: Michigan Protocol 2-5: Soft Tissue and Orthopedic Injuries
points: Pediatric patients (< 14 years of age): utilize MI MEDIC cards for appropriate medication dosage; when unavailable use pediatric dosing listed within protocol

Control bleeding per Bleeding Control (BCON)-Procedure Protocol: utilize direct pressure; consider early tourniquet use per Tourniquet Application-Procedure Protocol; consider MCA-approved hemostatic agents and hemorrhage control devices; consider pressure dressings with deep wound packing; consider pelvic binding for suspected unstable pelvic fracture
For uncontrolled bleeding with hemorrhagic shock: refer to Hemorrhagic Shock-Treatment Protocol
If appropriate, maintain spinal precautions per Spinal Injury Assessment-Treatment Protocol
Assess pain on 1–10 scale and treat per Pain Management-Procedure Protocol

Immobilize/splint orthopedic injuries as appropriate: consider traction splinting for closed femur fractures (excluding hip/femoral neck); straighten severely angulated fractures if distal extremity has signs of decreased perfusion; evaluate and document neurovascular status before and after splinting

Partial/complete amputations, major soft tissue injuries (mangled extremity), and open fractures: control bleeding; cover wounds with sterile dressings moistened with sterile solution; splint extremity; bring recoverable amputated parts to hospital as soon as possible; wrap amputated part in sterile dressing moistened with sterile solution — seal in plastic bag and if available place in container of ice and water — DO NOT place part directly on ice; obtain IV access (Specialist skill); administer antibiotics per MCA selection (Specialist skill); frequent monitoring of circulation, sensation, and motion distal to injury during transport
For severe crush injuries: refer to General Crush Injury-Treatment Protocol
Impaled objects are left in place and stabilized — removal only with approval of Medical Control
Provide pain management per Pain Management-Procedure Protocol

mca_selections_required.description: MCA must select antibiotic option for partial/complete amputations, open fractures, and major soft tissue injuries
options: Ceftriaxone Slow IV Push: 2 gm diluted with 20 mL NS — Adults: 2 gm diluted slow IVP 3–5 min; Pediatrics > 2 months: 50 mg/kg diluted slow IVP 3–5 min per MI MEDIC (max 2 gm)
Ceftriaxone Infusion: Diluted dose added to 100 mL NS bag — Adults: 2 gm diluted infuse over 15–30 min; Pediatrics > 7 years: per MI MEDIC or 50 mg/kg to 100 mL NS, max 2 gm, infuse over 15–30 min
Cefazolin Slow IV Push: 2 gm diluted with 20 mL NS — Adults: 2 gm diluted slow IVP 3–5 min; Pediatrics: per MI MEDIC or 30 mg/kg diluted slow IVP 3–5 min (max 2 gm)
Cefazolin Infusion: Diluted dose added to 100 mL NS bag — Adults: 2 gm diluted infuse over 15–30 min; Pediatrics > 7 years: per MI MEDIC or 30 mg/kg to 100 mL NS, max 2 gm, infuse over 15–30 min

key_drugs.name: Ceftriaxone (Open Fracture/Amputation)
key_drugs.dose: Adults: 2 gm diluted; Pediatrics > 2 months: 50 mg/kg per MI MEDIC (max 2 gm)
key_drugs.route: IV — slow IVP over 3–5 min or infusion in 100 mL NS over 15–30 min
key_drugs.indication: Open fractures, partial/complete amputations, major soft tissue injuries
key_drugs.notes: MCA must select administration method (slow IVP vs infusion). Per MI MEDIC for pediatric dosing.

key_drugs.name: Cefazolin (Open Fracture/Amputation)
key_drugs.dose: Adults: 2 gm diluted; Pediatrics: 30 mg/kg per MI MEDIC (max 2 gm)
key_drugs.route: IV — slow IVP over 3–5 min or infusion in 100 mL NS over 15–30 min
key_drugs.indication: Open fractures, partial/complete amputations, major soft tissue injuries
key_drugs.notes: MCA must select administration method. Per MI MEDIC for pediatric dosing.

deterioration_flags: Hemorrhagic shock signs — refer to Hemorrhagic Shock-Treatment Protocol; tourniquet or wound packing as indicated
Neurovascular compromise distal to injury (diminished/absent pulse, loss of sensation, motor deficit) — straighten angulated fractures if perfusion compromised; expedite transport
Pelvic instability with hemodynamic compromise — apply pelvic binder; refer to Hemorrhagic Shock protocol
scope_notes: Bleeding control, direct pressure, tourniquet: all levels
Splinting and traction splinting: all levels
Antibiotics (ceftriaxone/cefazolin): Specialist skill — per MCA selection
Pain management: refer to Pain Management-Procedure Protocol for scope by level
